text:
There's Jack who's always got time—

And yet tonight, no. You see,

He's lost. There's not a clue.

Around his ear is a tiny thing.

A charm! You can’t tell?

That's his birthday present.

It glows like a flying boat.

The charming thing just doesn't speak.

Jack thinks maybe it's a mouse—

In fact, he's sure it is—

But it's just a rock.

So off Jack goes searching—

Into his backyard—

A box buried under some leaves—

There is his charm!

It's a spider!

That's not a mouse?

No, a spider—

With big eyes—

What would it say?

"Ack!"

So Jack calls them all—

The Spiders. There's a whole family—

And they're quite good listeners—

Jack sits cross-legged beside them —

And just listens to the leaves rustling.

That's all they ever say—

Just leaves rustling—

Now Jack has a new friend.

After that
